Scenario: Scenario 1: Admin adds a new category
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.
Test data: Category Name: Power Tools; Category Description: Electric and battery-powered tools
Steps: the admin opens the category management page and enters a new category name.
Expected result: the category is added to the allowed categories list.; the new category appears in the listing creation dropdown for all members.; the system records the admin ID and timestamp.

Scenario: Scenario 2: Admin removes an existing category
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.; a category exists in the allowed categories list.; no ACTIVE tool listings currently use that category.
Test data: Category Name: Power Tools; Category Description: Electric and battery-powered tools
Expected result: the category is removed from the allowed categories list.; the category no longer appears in the listing creation dropdown.; existing deactivated listings that used the category retain it for history.

Scenario: Scenario 3: Admin cannot remove a category in use by active listings
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.; a category exists with one or more ACTIVE tool listings.
Test data: Category Name: Power Tools; Category Description: Electric and battery-powered tools
Steps: the admin attempts to remove the category.
Expected result: the system rejects the action.; a message lists the ACTIVE listings using that category.

Precondition: the logged-in user is not an admin.
Test data: Category Name: Power Tools; Category Description: Electric and battery-powered tools
Steps: the user attempts to access the category management page.
Expected result: the system returns 403 Forbidden and no changes are saved.

Scenario: Scenario 1: Admin generates a moderation report
Precondition: the logged-in user is an admin.
Test data: Date Range: 2026-06-01 to 2026-07-31; Report Type: Monthly moderation summary
Steps: the admin opens the reports page, selects a report type, selects a date range, and clicks Generate Report.
Expected result: the system displays a report with totals and relevant records for the selected criteria.

Scenario: Scenario 2: Admin exports a report as CSV
Test data: Date Range: 2026-06-01 to 2026-07-31; Report Type: Monthly moderation summary
Expected result: the data is downloaded as a .csv file whose contents match the on-screen data.; the CSV includes column headers matching the report columns.

Scenario: Scenario 3: Report has no matching data
Precondition: the admin selects a report type and date range where no matching records exist.
Test data: Date Range: 2026-06-01 to 2026-07-31; Report Type: Monthly moderation summary
Expected result: the system displays a message saying no matching records were found and does not generate an empty or misleading report.

Precondition: the logged-in user is not an admin.
Test data: Date Range: 2026-06-01 to 2026-07-31; Report Type: Monthly moderation summary
Steps: the user attempts to access the reports page.
Expected result: the system returns 403 Forbidden and no report data is shown.
